strengthen planning.instructions.md with explicit creation and tracking rules

Add seven explicit, actionable rules for how plan documents must be
created and tracked in this repo:

- Location rule: all plans must live under docs/
- Naming rule: kebab-case filenames ending in -plan.md
- README registration rule: every plan must be added to README.md
  Plans section immediately (required, not optional)
- Required sections rule: Goal/Purpose, Pre-Implementation Change
  Review, at least one Phase, Acceptance Criteria, Last updated marker
- Status marker rule: use ✅ Done / ⏳ Pending in headings; keep them
  current
- Completion rule: move README entry to Completed when all phases done
- Pre-implementation gate rule: Pre-Implementation Change Review is a
  blocking gate — agents must validate repo state before coding

Also adds a full plan template for copy-paste use when creating new
plans. Existing valid content (keep-plans-current guidance) retained
and updated to stay consistent.

* feat: GitHub Actions composite action, release decoupling, docs GitHub Actions section

action.yml (new):
- Composite action: download daemon → start → submit task → poll → stop
- Inputs: instruction/task_file, system_prompt (agency-agents chain),
  target_file, command, model, provider, openai/anthropic/copilot keys,
  timeout_seconds, start_daemon, daemon_url, nexus_version
- Outputs: task_id, status, logs
- Pure Python3 (no extra deps); cleanup step runs on always()

.github/workflows/release.yml:
- release job: needs [build] only (was [build, build-desktop])
- New release-desktop job: needs [build-desktop, release], if: always()
  && release succeeded && desktop builds succeeded
- Safer mv via find -maxdepth 1 -exec mv {} . instead of glob
- Explicit file list in release job (no wildcards)

docs/src/views/DownloadsView.vue:
- Desktop App cards: nexus-orchestrator-desktop-{os}-{arch}.* URLs
  (was pointing at CLI archives incorrectly)

docs/src/views/McpIntegrationView.vue:
- New #github-actions section: basic usage + agency-agents chaining example
- Commit step guarded by if: steps.nexus.outputs.status == 'COMPLETED'
- Key inputs reference table (12 rows)
- TOC entry added

commit.message:
fix: runtime crashes on PatientConsent, NetworkMemberships, HospitalNetworks

PatientConsent - v.map is not a function:
- listAnimals() returns { success: true, data: { animals: [...], total: N } }
- res?.data extracted the object not the array -> animals.map() crashed
- Fix: Array.isArray guard chain: res.data.animals -> res.data -> res -> []

NetworkMemberships - Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'id'):
- getMyEnrollments SQL used u_by.name which doesn't exist on users table
  (users has first_name + last_name only) -> 500 error -> silent state issue
- Fix: use u_by.first_name || ' ' || u_by.last_name AS enrolledByName
- Also added Array.isArray() guard on setEnrollments for extra safety

HospitalNetworks - patient.animals can be undefined:
- patient.animals.length and patient.animals.map crashed if animals missing
- Fix: (patient.animals ?? []).length and (patient.animals ?? []).map

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: summary agent now includes all agent types per symbol

get_recent_activities_by_symbol() was fetching TOP N activities per
symbol without grouping by agent_type. When a symbol had both put
and call watching, the most recent N activities could all be from
one type, leaving the other completely absent from summaries.

Changed to query each agent type (covered_call, cash_secured_put,
open_call_monitor, open_put_monitor) separately per symbol, then
merge and sort by timestamp. This ensures all active agent types
are represented in summary data.

commit.message:
Fix similar TooManySponsoring issue in revoke sponsorship

RevokeSponsorship's transfer and establish paths used ? on
update_num_sponsoring/apply_entry_sponsorship_with_sponsor, which
propagated TxError::Internal (becoming TxInternalError) instead of
returning OpTooManySponsoring when num_sponsoring was at u32::MAX.

Add pre-checks matching stellar-core's canTransferSponsorshipHelper and
canEstablishSponsorshipHelper pattern: verify num_sponsoring capacity
before mutating state. Affects all four sponsorship paths (ledger entry
transfer, ledger entry establish, signer transfer, signer establish).

commit.message:
Fix: skip stock for Grocy-import barcodes with no Grocy stock

During discover, queue entries with source='grocy-import' and
import_stock_amount=NULL now correctly skip adding stock (product had
0 stock in Grocy). Previously, Python's 'or 1.0' treated 0.0/NULL as
falsy and added 1 unit to every product regardless.

Non-import barcodes (scanner, manual, etc.) are unaffected and still
default to adding 1 unit when no explicit amount is provided.
